text: Last class you said you can't write a formal email yet.
Today you learn to write a clear, polite work email.
Recipient
Subject
Greeting
Attachment
Reply
Forward
Request
Regards
Polite
Draft
I am writing because I need the report.
The deadline is today, so please reply soon.
I sent the draft, but I have one question.
Please send the file. Also, confirm the date.
Hi Aline. I see you are writing an email to the client. Who is the recipient?
The recipient is the project manager. I added a clear subject and a polite greeting.
Good. Did you add the attachment with the report?
Yes, the attachment is the monthly report. I am writing because the client needs it today.
Perfect. How do you close the email?
I finish with a short request and the words Best regards. Then I reply to any questions.
Dear Mr. Costa, I hope you are well.
Could you please review the attachment?
It is urgent, so please reply today.
Thank you for your help. Best regards.

Hi Aline, this is Tom from the project team. I just sent you an important email, so please check your inbox. The subject is Monthly Report, and the attachment is the file with all the numbers. I am writing because the client needs our update before Friday. Please read it, and also forward it to your manager. If you have a question, just reply to me directly. I tried to keep the email short and polite. Thank you for your help, and talk soon.

This is a quick note to myself before I write my email. First, I choose a clear subject, so the recipient knows the topic. Then I start with a polite greeting, because first impressions matter. In the body, I write a short request, and I add the attachment with the report. I use simple linking words like and, but, and so to connect my ideas. Before I send, I read my draft one more time and check for mistakes. Finally, I close with Best regards, and I reply quickly to any answer. Writing a professional email is my new goal, and I feel ready.

First, Aline writes a clear subject, so the recipient knows the topic. Then she starts with a polite greeting. After that, she writes a short request in the body and adds the attachment. Next, she reads her draft and checks for mistakes. Finally, she closes with Best regards and sends the email.

Greeting
Attachment
Reply

Request
Regards
Polite
Draft
I am writing because I need the report.
It is urgent, so please reply today.
Could you please review the attachment?
I read my draft before I send it.
A work email should be short and polite.
I close with Best regards.
Dear Mr. Costa, I hope you are well.
Thank you for your help. Best regards.
